$schema: ./README.md (no formal schema yet; field set is canonical until v1)

bytecode.comment: 443-byte runtime size matches the audited artifact exactly. P6 audit (M-1) corrected an earlier value — P5-4's recording script used hashlib.sha3_256 (NIST SHA3-256) instead of Ethereum's Keccak-256; padding rules differ between the two so they produce different hashes for the same input. The hash recorded here now is Keccak-256 (the Ethereum primitive), independently verified via `cast keccak $(cast code 0x8566... --rpc-url ...)`. Live bytecode unchanged; only the recorded hash was wrong.

verification.comment: Get a free Basescan API key at https://basescan.org/myapikey, then run the manual command above. Verification just makes the source viewable on Basescan; deployed contract works regardless.

notes: Deployer wallet 0x89e7...c54 is Kelvin's dev/testing wallet only. It is NOT a user-facing device wallet. Per D-006, real user device wallets are generated client-side at install time and are unrelated to this address.
This is the canonical PoC RevisionLog reference until P5-1 is replaced by MVP-2 issue 2.1 (RevisionLog v1 on mainnet). v1 will live at a separate address with signature verification.
Contract is append-only by design: no admin, no upgrade, no pause, no selfdestruct. If broken, deploy v2 at a new address per the locked decision in §2 of the master plan.

RevisionLogV0_redeploy_proof.compiler: identical to RevisionLogV0 above (same Solidity source, same solc 0.8.24, same optimizer settings)

bytecode.comment: Same source compiled with the same toolchain produces the same bytecode, so the runtime keccak should match the canonical contract above. Verifiable post-deploy via `cast keccak $(cast code 0x74f2...c4 --rpc-url https://sepolia.base.org)`.

RevisionLogV0_redeploy_proof.purpose: P12 §3.9 PoC -> MVP gate criterion (4): proves redeploy-on-bug is real, not theoretical. The deploy script (contracts/script/DeployRevisionLogV0.s.sol) was re-run unchanged against the existing Foundry keystore and landed at a fresh address. This contract is NOT wired to any production code path; the canonical RevisionLogV0 above remains the active contract used by chaincli, pangolin-chain, and pangolin-cli.
notes: Recorded under D-015 in DECISIONS.md.
Identical gas profile (149135 gas at 0.006 gwei) to the original deploy at 0x8566... — same bytecode, same compiler artifact.
MVP-2 issue 2.1's RevisionLog v1 (with signature verification) will land at a third address; this redeploy is just for the PoC gate.

bytecode.comment: 1,825-byte runtime size matches the 2.1 plan-gate's audited artifact verbatim (well under EIP-170's 24,576 ceiling). Keccak hash is Ethereum Keccak-256 — independently verified via `cast keccak $(cast code 0x179362... --rpc-url https://sepolia.base.org)`.

verification.method: Basescan Standard-JSON-Input upload (manual, via web UI) on 2026-05-14
verification.comment: The deploy pipeline's auto-verify failed because foundry 1.0.0-stable (Feb 2025) pre-dates Etherscan's V2 multichain endpoint migration; the wrapper's --verifier-url config was switched to V2 in commit b421f95 but forge 1.0.0's URL builder strips the chainid query param. Workaround: generated Standard JSON Input via `forge verify-contract --show-standard-json-input ...` and uploaded via Basescan's UI. Future foundry-version bump will let the wrapper auto-verify directly.

notes: Recorded under D-017 in DECISIONS.md.

First MVP-2 contract — adds on-chain signature verification (ecrecover + EIP-712 typed-data) and a write-additive device-key registry over v0's append-only-immutable substrate. Per 2.1 R-a (Path B), the same keypair signs revisions AND pays gas (D-006).
No constructor arguments — V1 takes none. Deploy initcode is exactly the contract creation code.
v0 (D-014) stays append-only-immutable wherever it currently sits on chain; this v1 is the substrate every MVP-2 sync issue (3.1, 3.3, 4.1) points at.
Pangolin-chain adapter integration (-v1 domain prefix bump in `crates/pangolin-chain/src/signing.rs` + new secp256k1_signing.rs module) is a separate MVP-2 issue (3.1).

EntitlementRegistry_D018_smoke_test_supe.superseded_reason: Smoke-test placeholder with collapsed authorities (PAYMENT == REDEMPTION == pangolin-dev). MVP-2 issue 3.4 R-d required split-key production-grade redeploy; D-019 is the canonical EntitlementRegistry going forward. D-018 remains on chain (immutable; no admin / pause / selfdestruct) but is no longer referenced by any pangolin-chain constants.

bytecode.comment: 2,464-byte runtime size matches the 2.2 plan-gate's audited artifact verbatim (22,112-byte margin under EIP-170). Keccak hash is Ethereum Keccak-256.

constructor_args.comment: Per 2.3 R-b + 2.2 L8: both authority addresses collapsed to the pangolin-dev wallet for the testnet deploy. This collapses 2.2's split-trust property (R-a of 2.2); the testnet contract is for smoke-testing the integration only — no real money flows. Production-grade split keys ship with MVP-2 issue 3.4 (funder service) at a fresh redeployment.

verification.method: Basescan Standard-JSON-Input upload (manual, via web UI) on 2026-05-14
verification.comment: Same Etherscan-V2 migration constraint as D-017 — manual standard-json-input verification via Basescan's UI. Constructor args encoded inline (three 32-byte words). Future foundry-version bump will let the wrapper auto-verify directly.

notes: Recorded under D-018 in DECISIONS.md.
SUPERSEDED by D-019 on 2026-05-17 (split-key production-grade redeploy).
Per-user paid-balance ledger contract — funder-push split-signer (payment + redemption authorities) with strict-equality nonce + per-credit expiresAt. Same chain as RevisionLogV1 per D-008.

Constructor immutables locked at deploy: PAYMENT_AUTHORITY, REDEMPTION_AUTHORITY (both pangolin-dev on testnet per R-b), initial schemaVersion=1. No setSigner / setAuthority / rotateKeys — a compromised key would require a v2 redeploy per 2.2 R-d.

THIS WAS THE SMOKE-TEST INSTANCE. Production deployment with real payment-processor + funder-service split keys shipped 2026-05-17 as D-019 at 0xdDa04e427e95e50Cfd22703A76CAE2E1Da4F5fCD; the canonical EntitlementRegistry entry in this file is now D-019.

bytecode.audited_at: 2.2 SIGNOFF (commit 26b7af1; identical bytecode to D-018 since contract source is unchanged)
bytecode.comment: Same bytecode as D-018 (identical EntitlementRegistry.sol source; the deploy difference is purely constructor args). Keccak hash is Ethereum Keccak-256 verified via `cast keccak $(cast code 0xdDa04e427e95e50Cfd22703A76CAE2E1Da4F5fCD --rpc-url https://sepolia.base.org)`.

constructor_args.comment: D-019 split-key production-grade redeploy. PAYMENT_AUTHORITY = pangolin-dev (issues Credit purchase attestations; same wallet that signed D-018 + D-017). REDEMPTION_AUTHORITY = pangolin-funder-dev (NEW keystore created 2026-05-17 for the funder service's signing key; signs redemption authorizations dispensed alongside ETH top-ups per 3.4 funder lifecycle). Production-grade key separation per 2.2 R-a + 3.4 R-d; closes the D-018 collapsed-authority smoke-test posture.

domain_separator.comment: Pinned at source in crates/pangolin-chain/src/secp256k1_signing.rs as ENTITLEMENT_DOMAIN_SEPARATOR_BASE_SEPOLIA_V1. The redemption_domain_separator_matches_pinned_constant test catches drift at CI time.

verification.method: Basescan Standard-JSON-Input upload via web UI (operational follow-up; same workaround D-017 + D-018 used)
verification.comment: Auto-verify failed in the wrapper (BASESCAN_API_KEY env var was not set at deploy time). Get a free Basescan API key at https://basescan.org/myapikey then run the manual command above OR upload via Basescan's web UI. Verification is cosmetic; the deployed contract works regardless.

notes: Recorded under D-019 in DECISIONS.md.
D-019 split-key production-grade redeploy (2026-05-17) — SUPERSEDES D-018 (the smoke-test instance with collapsed authorities).

Constructor immutables locked at deploy: PAYMENT_AUTHORITY = pangolin-dev (0x89e7...5c54), REDEMPTION_AUTHORITY = pangolin-funder-dev (0xaeE7...55AC, NEW keystore created 2026-05-17 funded with 0.03 ETH from pangolin-dev via tx 0xe8ec3b5632...). initial schemaVersion = 1. No setSigner / setAuthority / rotateKeys — a compromised key would require a v2 redeploy per 2.2 R-d.
Per-user paid-balance ledger contract — funder-push split-signer (payment + redemption authorities) with strict-equality nonce + per-credit expiresAt. Same chain as RevisionLogV1 per D-008.

Production-grade split-key separation closes the D-018 smoke-test posture per 3.4 R-d; pangolin-chain constants (EXPECTED_ENTITLEMENT_REGISTRY_ADDRESS_BASE_SEPOLIA + ENTITLEMENT_DOMAIN_SEPARATOR_BASE_SEPOLIA_V1) updated in the same commit that records this entry.
